2.  **Identify Key Concepts:**
   - Binary Search Tree (BST): Binary tree where each node has at most 2 children, left child < parent < right child, typically used for in-memory data structures.
   - B-tree: Self-balancing tree designed for systems that read/write large blocks of data (like databases/filesystems), each node can have multiple children and keys, optimized for disk I/O.
   - Key differences: Structure/node capacity, balancing mechanism, use cases, performance characteristics, memory/disk optimization, search/insert
